critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

C1 — ACA COMMUNITY RATING CONFUSION: Community rating rules restrict individual premium differentiation. The concern is whether the cost model's outputs flow into premium decisions.

C2 — PRIOR-YEAR CLAIMS PROXY: Health status proxy in any model connected to pricing raises regulatory flags.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (responding to critic):

C1 — The model's use case is explicitly internal: cost prediction and reserve setting. ACA community rating rules apply to member-facing premium rates, which are set separately using only permissible rating factors. Outside counsel reviewed and confirmed this distinction.

C2 — Prior-year claims is appropriate for internal cost prediction. ACA restrictions on health status apply only to individual premium determination, not to internal actuarial reserve modeling. The model does not determine premiums.
adjudication_raw: Defense wins both points. ACA community rating rules do not apply to internal actuarial models used for reserve setting and reinsurance assessment. Outside counsel confirmation provides strong support for this position.
